name: Flank Steak with Garlic Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: Salt and black pepper to taste
Garlic puree (from 1 roasted head of garlic)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the flank steak generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
2. Heat a wide heavy skillet over medium-high heat without adding any fat.
3. When hot, cook the seasoned steak until it is well seared and browned on each side for about 1 minute per side.
4. Reduce the heat to medium and add the softened butter. Cook for an additional 3 to 5 minutes on each side.
5. For best results, serve the meat quite rare. Remove the meat from the pan and keep it warm.
6. Pour off the fat in the skillet and add the scallions and red wine. Bring to a boil and whisk in the garlic puree.
7. Continue boiling until the wine has reduced by half, thickened, and becomes syrupy.
8. As the sauce boils, scrape up any browned bits from the skillet with a wooden spoon.
9. Stir in the accumulated meat juices under the flank steak.
10. Boil for an additional 1 to 2 seconds.
11. Remove from heat. Gently swirl in the softened butter so it incorporates into the wine sauce.
12. Slice the meat against the grain into thin strips and arrange on a hot platter.
13. Pour the sauce down the center of the slices and serve immediately.
